policy: The reader manuscript must remain complete in third-person prose without these prompts. These records only preserve places where Corben may later add author-supplied first-person experience, project history, conviction, or cadence. Codex must not fabricate that material and no release approval depends on these prompts being answered.

records.optional_author_prompt: Corben should supply the plain-language reason this architecture matters and the reader promise the opening must carry.
records.curation_boundary: Optional author-supplied first-person or project-history material may enrich a later edition, but the current reader manuscript must stand in finished third-person prose without this input and may not claim Corben experience unless he supplies it.

records.optional_author_prompt: Corben should decide which real project pressures made authority boundaries and failure obligations feel non-negotiable.
records.curation_boundary: Optional author-supplied first-person or project-history material may enrich a later edition, but the current reader manuscript must stand in finished third-person prose without this input and may not claim Corben experience unless he supplies it.

non_claims: This queue is not a reader release record.
This queue does not approve EPUB, PDF, DOCX, HTML, audio, or audio-embedded EPUB artifacts.
This queue does not promote any claim support state.
This queue does not permit agent-written first-person authorial experience.
This queue does not make author input a blocker for finished third-person reader prose.
